title: Macramé wreath frame hanger
subtitle: A decorative hanger for a 25 cm wreath frame, using gathered knot clusters and trailing fringe in 4 mm cotton cord
excerpt: A knotted hanger that cradles a 25 cm wreath ring in a V-shaped spread of square knots and diagonal double half-hitch lines. Extends 30 cm above the wreath for wall mounting. Six trailing cords hang as fringe below. 12 cords of 4 mm cotton macramé cord, folded double.
sourceNotes: Macramé wreath frame hangers are a contemporary decorative project using established macramé techniques. Format, cord calculations, and construction sequence are original. Not drawn from a specific published source.

glossaryTerms.definition: A symmetrical flat knot made by crossing two working cords alternately left-over-right then right-over-left around a pair of filler cords. The standard unit of macramé fabric.
Two half-hitch loops tied consecutively with the same working cord around a carrier cord. Used to build diagonal lines, chevrons, and curves in macramé.
A wrapping knot in which one cord winds tightly around a bundle of cords, then the tail threads through the wraps and is pulled to lock them. Consolidates multiple cords into a single grouped point.
A folded-cord mounting knot. The cord is folded in half, the loop placed over a ring or dowel, and both tails pulled through the loop.
The finished outer edge of a woven or knotted panel. In macramé, the selvedge cords are the outermost tails, often incorporated into half-knots to keep the edge tidy.

content.text: This hanger is designed to display a 25 cm wreath ring on a wall hook or door knob. The upper section extends 30 cm above the wreath and forms a V-shaped spread of
square knots
and diagonal
double half-hitch
lines that meet the wreath ring from both sides, cradling it without additional fixing. Below the wreath ring, 6 trailing cords hang as fringe. Cords are mounted on a small ring at the top with
lark's head knots
, consolidated above with a
gathering knot
, and the
selvedge
cords that drape below the wreath become the trailing fringe. Cord: 4 mm cotton macramé cord, 12 cords cut to 2.5 m each.

items.name: 4 mm single-twist cotton macramé cord — 12 cords, each cut to 2.5 m, giving 1.25 m working length when folded double
Small metal ring, 3 cm diameter — for the top mounting loop
Wreath ring or frame, 25 cm diameter — the display frame (wire, willow, or floral)
Macramé fringe comb (optional, for softening the trailing fringe)

content.text: Cord cutting

Cut 12 lengths of 4 mm single-twist cotton macramé cord, each 2.5 m long. When folded in half and mounted on the top ring, each cord contributes two working tails of 1.25 m. With 12 cords mounted, you have 24 working tails. Of these: 18 tails form the knotted upper section and the cradle arms; 6 tails are left as trailing fringe below the wreath ring. Label the centre 6 tails (tails 10 to 15 counting from the left) with a clip or knot tie: these become the fringe.
Setting up

Fold each of the 12 cords in half and mount them onto the small top ring with lark's head knots, sitting the knots flush against each other around the ring. Pin the ring to the top of the macramé board. The 24 tails hang below in a flat spread of roughly 15 cm width. Pin the outer tails to the board edges so the spread stays open.

Tie a gathering knot immediately below the top ring to consolidate all 24 tails into a single column. Take a 30 cm length of 4 mm cord and fold it in half with the loop pointing upward. Wind the long tail tightly around all 24 cord tails six times, then thread the winding tail through the upward loop and pull the top of the short cord to close the loop through the wraps. The gathering knot should sit 1 to 2 cm below the ring.
The pattern

definition.caption: Double half-hitch: each working cord ties two consecutive half-hitches around the carrier cord

content.text: Section 1: Upper column (5 cm below the gathering knot)

Working with all 24 tails as a flat group, tie two rows of alternating square knots in a 3 cm section: Row 1 groups the tails in sets of 4 (6 knots across); Row 2 offsets by 2 cords (5 knots). This produces a short flat panel that forms the upper trunk of the hanger.
Section 2: V-spread (12 to 15 cm below the gathering knot)

Divide the 24 tails into two equal groups of 12: left group (tails 1 to 12) and right group (tails 13 to 24). Work each group separately to create the V-shaped arms that spread outward to the wreath ring. On the left group: take tail 1 as the carrier cord and hold it diagonally from upper left to lower right at approximately 45 degrees. Using tails 2 to 12 in sequence, tie one double half-hitch per tail around the carrier. This creates a diagonal line running outward to the left. Add a second diagonal line 2 cm below by repeating with tail 2 as the carrier. Tie two rows of square knots in the left group below the diagonal lines. On the right group: mirror the process, running the diagonal carrier from upper right to lower left.
Section 3: Attaching to the wreath ring

Hold the wreath ring at the intended position, approximately 30 cm below the top ring. Bring the left arm of the V across the wreath ring and secure it with a gathering knot: gather the 12 left-group tails together, hold them over and behind the wreath ring wire, then tie a gathering knot around the tails against the ring. The ring should sit inside the knot with the tails locked on either side. Repeat for the right arm. This cradles the wreath ring between the two gathered cord clusters without needing adhesive or additional hardware.
Finishing

After gathering both arms onto the wreath ring, all 24 tails now hang below the ring. The centre 6 tails (identified earlier) are left as fringe. The remaining 18 tails on the outer sides can be trimmed to 4 to 5 cm and the ends slightly frayed with the fringe comb, or folded back behind the wreath ring and secured with a half-hitch to tidy them out of sight.

For the trailing fringe: comb the 6 centre tails downward with the macramé fringe comb or a stiff brush, separating the individual plies. Trim to a uniform length of 12 to 15 cm below the wreath ring. The fringe can be left as combed cotton fluff or trimmed to a clean straight edge. To hang the completed hanger, pass the top ring over a wall hook or door knob.

items.problem: The wreath ring tilts rather than hanging level
items.cause: The two gathering knots that hold the ring are at different heights on the cord arms
items.fix: Before tying the second gathering knot, measure from the top ring to the intended ring position on both arms and mark with a T-pin. Tie both gathering knots at the same measured distance.

items.problem: The V-arms pull the wreath ring upward when the hanger is weighted
items.cause: The diagonal double half-hitch lines are tied with the carrier at too steep an angle, shortening the arm effectively
items.fix: Redo the diagonal rows at a shallower angle (60 to 70 degrees rather than 45 degrees). This leaves more cord length in the arms to span the 30 cm drop to the wreath ring.

items.problem: The gathering knot slips off the cord bundle at the wreath ring attachment
items.cause: The 4 mm cord surface is smooth and the wrap is not tight enough to generate friction
items.fix: Increase wrap count to 10 or 12 and pull the locking tail through the loop twice. Alternatively, tie a square knot over the gathering knot wraps for additional security.
